keyPoints: Project planning involves defining scope, schedule, budget, and resources to ensure a project is delivered on time, within budget, and meets requirements.
Quality management involves identifying and prioritizing quality metrics, such as defect density and test coverage, to ensure the software meets the required standards.

Project planning helps in risk management by identifying potential risks and developing mitigation strategies, while quality management ensures that defects are identified and addressed early in the development process.
Quality management also involves continuous improvement by monitoring software quality metrics and making data-driven decisions to improve the quality of the software.
By integrating project planning and quality management, software development teams can ensure that the software is delivered on time, within budget, and meets the required quality standards.
